zh.West Longdon, Leicestershire, England: 英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾韋斯特 蘭頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當William de Saye於大約1065年出生在法國諾門迪時，他的父親Robert fitz Picot de Say Lord of Clun, Vicomte21歲，母親Unnamed Wife de Saye17歲。他於大約1065年與Agnes Grandmesnil在法國諾門迪結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1144年在英國素可哈姆享年80歲。

zh-hans.West Longdon, Leicestershire, England: 英国英格兰萊希斯特斯爾韋斯特 蘭頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William de Saye于大约1065年出生在法国諾門迪时，他的父亲Robert fitz Picot de Say Lord of Clun, Vicomte21岁，母亲Unnamed Wife de Saye17岁。他于大约1065年与Agnes Grandmesnil在法国諾門迪结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于1144年在英国素可哈姆享年80岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Saye
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of Slovenian Saje: nickname for someone with very dark, sooty hair, derived from the root of sajast ‘sooty’, or directly from saja ‘soot’.
West African (Liberia): from the personal name Saye, found among the Mano (Mah) people, denoting a first-born boy.

variants.name: Say
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Sai in Orne or Say in Indre, France, perhaps so called from a Gaulish personal name Saius + the Latin locative suffix -acum (see also French name 3 below).

English: nickname, perhaps for someone who made or sold say, a type of woollen cloth, or for an habitual wearer of clothes made of this material, from Middle English sai(e) ‘say’ (Old French saie, from Latin saga, plural of sagum ‘military cloak’). See also Sayer .

French (southern): topographic name from saix (from Latin saxum) ‘rock’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word, for example, Say in Loire, Saix in Tarn and Vienne, Le Saix in Hautes-Alpes, or Les Saix in Isère.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When William de Saye was born about 1065, in Normandy, France, his father, Robert fitz Picot de Say Lord of Clun, Vicomte, was 21 and his mother, Unnamed Wife de Saye, was 17. He married Agnes Grandmesnil about 1065, in Normandy, France. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died in 1144, in Hamsey, Sussex, England, at the age of 80.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William de Saye est né environ 1065, à Normandie, France, son père, Robert fitz Picot de Say Lord of Clun, Vicomte, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Unnamed Wife de Saye, avait 17 ans. Il a épousé Agnes Grandmesnil environ 1065, à Normandie, France. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé en 1144, à Hamsey, Sussex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 80 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: William de Saye(이)가 대략 1065년 프랑스 노먼디에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert fitz Picot de Say Lord of Clun, Vicomte(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Unnamed Wife de Saye(은)는 17세였다. 그는 대략 1065년 프랑스 노먼디에서 Agnes Grandmesnil(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1144년 영국 수세스 함시에서 80세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William de Saye родился около 1065 г. в местности норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени Robert fitz Picot de Say Lord of Clun, Vicomte было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Unnamed Wife de Saye, 17. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Grandmesnil около 1065 г. в местности норманди, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер в 1144 г. в местности хамси, суссекс, Англия в возрасте 80 лет.
